executiveSummary: KCE_Demo is a three-tier reference application: a React/Vite single-page dashboard, a FastAPI service, and a PostgreSQL database, wired together over a small REST surface. The dashboard reads backend health and the current item catalog on load; the service exposes three endpoints that report health and list or create catalog items backed by a single 'items' table. The functional graph captures two actors: the Dashboard Viewer who consumes the read-only dashboard, and the System that owns the API and persistence. Scope is deliberately narrow, this is a demo stack, not a production product.
keyBusinessObjectives: Demonstrate an end-to-end React + FastAPI + PostgreSQL slice that runs fully offline via Docker Compose
Expose a minimal REST contract (health, list items, create item) that the frontend consumes directly
Persist catalog items durably in PostgreSQL through SQLAlchemy
Surface backend connectivity status to the user on dashboard load

keyStakeholders.interest: Wants to see at a glance whether the backend is reachable and what items currently exist, without any edit controls.
Owns the REST contract and the items table; responsible for serving reads, accepting writes, and reporting health.

keyCapabilities: Read-only dashboard rendering of status and item list with empty-state handling
PostgreSQL persistence of the items table through SQLAlchemy

System.description: The FastAPI backend service. Exposes the REST contract, owns the SQLAlchemy-mapped 'items' table in PostgreSQL, and reports its own liveness.

Dashboard Viewer.description: An end user of the React/Vite SPA. Opens the dashboard to view backend connectivity and the current catalog; the UI exposes no create or edit controls.

8b00b96e-e912-470b-aa25-fd29b44228b3.businessValue: Provides the entire server-side contract the demo depends on: a liveness signal, a paginated read path, and a single write path, all backed by durable PostgreSQL storage.
8b00b96e-e912-470b-aa25-fd29b44228b3.mermaidDiagram:
graph TD
  subgraph System[FastAPI Backend]
    H[GET / health]
    L[GET /api/items list]
    C[POST /api/items create]
  end
  L -->|read| DB[(items table)]
  C -->|insert| DB

cdf46510.businessValue: Gives the user a single read-only view that confirms backend connectivity and shows the current item catalog, with an explicit empty state when no items exist.
cdf46510.mermaidDiagram:
graph TD
  V[Dashboard Viewer] --> APP[App on mount]
  APP -->|GET /| ST[Backend status card]
  APP -->|GET /api/items| LI[Item list / empty state]
